NORW AY . 2 Mark, 1661. Christiania Mint. Frederik III. NGC AU Details—Cleaned. KM-31; Hede-67D; Sieg-6.4; 
NMD-161A; Schou-42; Bruun-9869. Weight: 10.90 gms. Mintmaster: Frederik Grüner. Nicely struck and impressively 
pedigreed example of one of the rarest of all the 2 Marks from Frederik III. The rarity was underlined by F . C. Bech by 
putting an ink cross in the obverse right field.

Extremely Rare and Attractive Half Taler
12095
NORW AY . 1/2 Speciedaler, 1671. Christiania Mint. Christian V . NGC Unc Details—Obverse Cleaned. KM-107; 
Hede-33; Sieg-47; NMD-69B; Schou-14; Bruun-10114. Weight: 14.44 gms. Mintmaster: Frederik Grüner. Engraver: Jo-
nas Olufsen Lunder. Extremely rare and attractive 1/2 Speciedaler of the so-called “large head” variant. From the whole 
reign of Christian V , 1/2 Speciedalers were only minted in four years (1671, 1673, 1674 and 1693), the first three that 
were minted at Christiania are all exceedingly rare and highly coveted by all collectors of Norwegian 17th century coins.
€20,000-€25,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
Ex: Joh. G. Guildal Collection (Private Sale - 1918); later published by the Danish Numismatic Society as item 1709 in his collec-
tion.

Page 198 Stack’s Bowers Galleries
The L.  E. Bruun Col lection • A Corpus of Scandinavian Monetary History Part III
One of Only Two Recorded Examples
12106
NORW AY . Gold Off-metal strike Mark, 1685. Christiania Mint. Christian V . NGC VF-35. Fr-Unlisted; KM-cf. Pn30 
(silver); Hede-18; Sieg-80; JS-N2190; Schou-1; Bruun-10272. Weight: 5.19 gms. Mintmaster: Peter Grüner the Y ounger. 
The only other example in existence, weighing 7.20 gms. is in the Danish National Museum. The varying weights sug-
gest that it is not a regular gold coin but rather an off-strike of an unknown Mark from the so-called “Dannebrog-series” . 
A both interesting and mysterious piece that will be a spectacular addition to any collection of Norwegian coins.
€15,000-€20,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
    
12107
NORW AY . Mark, 1686. Christiania Mint. Christian V . NGC AU-58. KM-146; Hede-57B; Sieg-25.2; NMD-124; 
Schou-24; Bruun-10288. Weight: 5.11 gms. Mintmaster: Peter Grüner the Y ounger. Exceptionally nicely struck for type 
and with a lovely toning and plenty of luster, this piece is bound to attract attention. 
€2,500-€3,500
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
Ex: Ove T. Thomsen Collection (Copenhagen - 1880) Lot # 3652.

AU-55. KM-162; Hede-69B; Sieg-26.2; NMD-212; Schou-36; 
Bruun-10321. Weight: 5.43 gms. Mintmaster: Henning Christo-
pher Meyer the Elder. Pleasantly toned and sharply struck one 
year-type. 
€2,500-€3,500
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
    
12111
NORW AY . Krone, 1688-HCM. Kongsberg Mint. Christian V . 
NGC EF-40. Dav-3666; KM-183; Hede-73; Sieg-42; NMD-182B; 
Schou-14; Bruun-10336. Weight: 22.06 gms. Mintmaster: Hen-
ning Christopher Meyer the Elder. Light flan flaw on obverse. 
Nice luster and a fine, warm toning. Scarce first date of this type 
that was minted every year from 1688 to 1699. 
€2,200-€3,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
Ex: Countship of Brahesminde Collection (Private Sale - 1922).
Ex: Peter v. Hemmert Collection (Copenhagen - 1807) Lot # 1290.
    
12112

NMD-127; Schou-19; Bruun-10358. Weight: 3.98 gms. Mint -
master: Peter Grüner the Y ounger. Scarcely found this detailed.
Assumedly struck as a token to be used for payment of a ride of

the Summer.

========== PAGE 219 ==========

 Bid Online at www.StacksBowers.com Pa ge 201
Session II • Thursday , October 30, 2025 • 2:00  PM CET / 6:00  AM PT
Near-Choice Speciedaler with Edge Lettering in German
12115
NORW AY . Speciedaler, 1692. Kongsberg Mint. Christian V . NGC MS-62. Dav-3659E; KM-190; Hede-37G; Sieg-59.5; 
NMD-168E; Schou-13; Bruun-10393. Mintmaster: Henning Christopher Meyer the Elder. Engraver: Caspar Barth. The 
edge bears the following lettering: NICHT AUS SILBER - SUCHT DIESE NORDENS - FRUCHT WIRD ZU GOTTES 
EHR GESUCHT. A phenomenally inviting example with a very attractive toning and a wonderful luster. The two very 
light crosses that can just be recognized in the obverse right field was put there by F . C. Bech as an indication that the 
coin was “perrar” (very rare), and it is indeed with NMD having only recorded 6 examples.
By far most of the Speciedalers struck in Denmark/Norway in the 17th century were not made for regular circulation but for 
use as presentation pieces or savings. The Norwegian Speciedalers with lettered edges are however notable exceptions, as a 
total of almost 100,000 is known to have been minted from 1692-1696. As opposed to being hammer struck, the Kongsberg 
Speciedalers were minted in a screw press after the lettering had already been applied to the planchets, which means that 
the reading direction and the starting point of the legends vary. There are seven known varieties of edge legends of which 
five are in Danish, one is in German and one is in Latin. They were all meant to glorify the Danish absolute monarch and 
celebrate the important occurrence of silver in the Norwegian cliffs around the new mint in Kongsberg that had taken over 
from Christiania in 1686. The phrases, which are extraordinarily high-flown and poetical, are believed to be by the vicar 
Jørgen Kongstad.
€10,000-€13,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
Ex: Frederik Chr. Bech Collection (Copenhagen - 1906) Lot # 793.

========== PAGE 220 ==========

Page 202 Stack’s Bowers Galleries
The L.  E. Bruun Col lection • A Corpus of Scandinavian Monetary History Part III
    
12116
NORW AY . Speciedaler, 1693. Kongsberg Mint. Christian V . 
NGC AU Details—Cleaned. Dav-3660C; KM-192; Hede-40C; 
Sieg-60.3; NMD-170C; Schou-18; Bruun-10412. Mintmaster: 
Henning Christopher Meyer the Elder. Engraver: Caspar Barth. 
The edge bears the following lettering: DANNER KONGIS 
NORDSKE FIELDE SLIGE FRUGTER HAR I V ÆLDE. (“The 
Danish king’s Norwegian cliffs abound with such fruits”). A very 
handsome piece with clear details and a subtle re-toning after 
a light cleaning. All three edge variant of this one year type are 
very rare and sought after. 
€6,000-€8,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
Ex: Countship of Brahesminde Collection (Private Sale - 1922).
Ex: C. D. von Kreber Collection (Copenhagen - 1841) Lot # 1399.
    
12117
NORW AY . Speciedaler, 1694. Kongsberg Mint. Christian V . 
NGC MS-60. Dav-3661B; KM-194; Hede-39B; Sieg-61.2; NMD-
172B; Schou-11; Bruun-10424. Weight: 28.78 gms. Mintmaster: 
Henning Christopher Meyer the Elder. Engraver: Caspar Barth. 
The edge bears the following lettering: DET KLIPPERNE YDER 
VOR BERGMAND UDBRYDER HV AD HYTTEN DA GYDER 
AF MYNTEN VI NYDER (“Hidden in the hills, mined by our 
men, flowing from the ovens, the silver is our wealth”). With its 
admirable luster and delicate toning the present piece is most 
likely the finest of the relatively few in private ownership. 
€6,000-€8,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
Ex: Countship of Brahesminde Collection (Private Sale - 1922).

NORW AY . Speciedaler, 1695. Kongsberg Mint. Christian

NMD-174C; Schou-5; Bruun-10446. Weight: 28.88 gms. Mint-
master: Henning Christopher Meyer the Elder. Engraver: Cas -
par Barth. The edge bears the following lettering: DANNER 
KONGIS NORDSKE FIELDE SLIGE FRUGTER HAR I V ÆLDE 
(“The Danish king’s Norwegian cliffs abound with such fruits”). 
A rare variant with tremendous eye-appeal. 
€6,000-€8,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
    
12119

NMD-205B; Schou-21; Bruun-10453. Weight: 11.20 gms. Mint-
master: Henning Christopher Meyer the Elder. Although at 
some point very lightly cleaned, the piece emanates with a soft 
luster and excellent details. Extremely rare.

Marvelous Taler with Extremely Rare Edge Lettering
Presumably the Only Example Outside the Danish National Museum
12120
NORW AY . Speciedaler, 1696. Kongsberg Mint. Christian V . NGC AU Details—Cleaned. Dav-3661; KM-194; Hede-
39F; Sieg-61.6; NMD-176F; Schou-17; Bruun-10464. Weight: 28.47 gms. Mintmaster: Henning Christopher Meyer the 
Elder. Engraver: Caspar Barth. The edge bears the following very rare lettering: STORE KONGE NORDENS ÆRE LAD 
DE FRUGTER YNDIG V ÆRE SOM DIG NORSKE KLIPPER BÆRE (“Great king, glory of the North, enjoy the fruits 
that the Norwegian cliffs bear”). As always with a von Hemmert pedigree the condition is beyond reproach and the 
strike itself indicates that this piece was made as a presentation piece. One of only two examples traced, the other one 
being in the National Museum of Denmark.
€15,000-€18,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
Ex: Countship of Brahesminde Collection (Private Sale - 1922).
Ex: Peter v. Hemmert Collection (Copenhagen - 1807) Lot # 1422.

Exceedingly Rare and Captivating
12122
NORW AY . 1/2 Ducat, ND. Christiania mint. Christian V . NGC MS-61. Fr-10; KM-103; Hede-6; Sieg-62; NMD-21A; 
Schou-40; Bruun-10510. Weight: 1.74 gms. Mintmaster: Peter Grüner the Y ounger. Exceedingly rare and presenting 
exceptionally well with plenty of vibrant luster and superior details. As opposed to other of the Norwegian gold pieces 
struck for Christian V that sometimes have irregular weights, this was undoubtedly struck for circulation. 
€20,000-€25,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
Possibly ex: Countship of Brahesminde Collection (Private Sale - 1922).
    
12123
NORW AY . 2 Mark, 1700. Kongsberg Mint. Frederik IV . NGC MS-61. KM-200.1; Hede-2; Sieg-8; NMD-8; Schou-8; 
Kold-172; Bruun-10518. Weight: 8.92 gms. Mintmaster: Henning Christopher Meyer the Elder. Engraver: Caspar Barth. 
The edge carries the following Latin inscription: SERV ANT ET DECORANT (“Protecting and decorating”). A ravishing 
example of this Meybusch inspired one-year type.
Before entering the collection of count Bille-Brahe, it was owned by Peter Frederik Suhm (1728-1798). Suhm was out of a 
Danish noble family and a career at court would have been the obvious choice. He was however keenly interested in history 
and studied at the University of Copenhagen where he was fortunate enough to have the famous author, Ludvig Holberg as a 
teacher. He went on to write a number of important historical works and became chairman of the Danish Academy of Scienc-
es. He was also a phenomenal book collector and his collection of about 100,000 books are now in the Danish Royal Library. 
€4,000-€5,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
Ex: Countship of Brahesminde Collection (Private Sale - 1922).
Ex: Peter Frederik Suhm Collection (Copenhagen - 1802) Lot # 2110.

========== PAGE 224 ==========

Page 206 Stack’s Bowers Galleries
The L.  E. Bruun Col lection • A Corpus of Scandinavian Monetary History Part III
Enticing and Legendary Rarity
12125
NORW AY . 24 Skilling (Pattern), 1714. Kongsberg Mint. Frederik IV . NGC AU-55. KM-Pn37; Hede-13; Sieg-18; 
NMD-88; Schou-4; Bruun-10591. Weight: 4.56 gms. Mintmaster: Henning Christopher Meyer the Elder. Engraver: 
Anders Jonsen Lunde. A most enticing piece with a nice, old cabinet toning and impeccably detailed. Together with the 
48 Skilling of the same date and design, this pattern is of the utmost numismatic importance. The rarity is legendary and 
this is easily the most desirable of all Norwegian coins from the reign of Frederik IV . Only two examples are known of 
both denominations of which this is the only one to be privately held.
€20,000-€30,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
Ex: Countship of Brahesminde Collection (Private Sale - 1922).
    
12124
NORW AY . 8 Skilling (Pattern), 1711. Kongsberg Mint. Frederik IV . NGC EF Details—Cleaned. KM-Pn35; Hede-9; 
Sieg-15; NMD-89B; Schou-18; Bruun-10574. Weight: 2.12 gms. Mintmaster: Henning Christopher Meyer the Elder. En-
graver: Andreas Johnsen Lunde. Rare “Wildmen” pattern with nice details and traces of original luster. 
€2,500-€3,500
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.

NORW AY . 2 Skilling, 1742. Kongsberg Mint. Christian VI. 
NGC MS-64. KM-235; H-5; Sieg-1; NMD-24; Schou-5; Bru -
un-10695. Weight: 1.14 gms. Mintmaster: Truls Andersen Lyng.

12128
NORW AY . 24 Skilling, 1753. Kongsberg Mint. Christian 
VI. NGC EF-40. KM-236; H-2A; Sieg-3.1; NMD-10; Schou-4; 
Bruun-10726. Weight: 14.05 gms. Mintmaster: Truls Andersen 
Lyng. Engraver: Erasmus Simon Kongsberg. A very attractive 
and highly interesting and unique example clearly overstruck on 
an English Halfcrown from William III, struck in Bristol 1696 or 
1697, and thereby weighing more than 50% above the ordained 
weight. 
€3,000-€4,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
    
12129
DENMARK. Speciedaler, 1769. Rethwisch Mint. Christian 
VII. NGC AU Details—Reverse Cleaned. Dav-1304; KM-603; 
Hede-6; Sieg-34; Schou-8; FP-R3; Bruun-10781. Weight: 28.88 
gms. Mintmaster: Hans Schierven Knoph. Engraver: Johan 
Ephraim Bauert. An immensely lustrous and choicely struck ex-
ample boasting a magnificent toning with a plethora of shades of 
blue. The emission of this type was very brief, as the king on the 
11th of March 1769 decided that the reverse type should be re -
placed with the Danish Arms of three lions. Only two dates were 
struck, 1768 and 1769 with the 1769 being excessively rare. The 
fact that the Norwegian Lion had been used on the reverse made 
H. H. Schou list it under Norway in the 1928 Bruun catalog.
This piece has an especially rare and interesting pedigree having 
belonged to the Countess Georgine von Buchwaldt Moltke. After 
her death in 1808 her husband, Joachim Godske Moltke (Prime 
Minister of Denmark from 1814 - 1818) decided that her coin col-
lection should be sold and the proceeds should be offered as a do-
nation for the reconstruction of the Danish Fleet that had been 
commandeered by the English in 1807 during the Napoleonic wars.
€4,000-€6,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
Ex: Countship of Brahesminde Collection (Private Sale - 1922).
Ex: Georgine Moltke Collection (Copenhagen - 1811) Lot # 871.
    
12130
NORW AY . 24 Skilling, 1783. Kongsberg Mint. Christian VII. 
NGC MS-62. KM-250; H-12B; Sieg-11.2; NMD-36; Schou-10; 
FP-23.2; Bruun-10833. Weight: 9.16 gms. Mintmaster: Hans Ja-
cob Arnold Branth. Engraver: Andersen Jonsen Lunde. A scarce 
date rarely found in Mint State condition. 
€1,500-€2,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.

12131
NORW AY . Gold Off-Metal Strike 8 Skilling, 1783. Kongsberg 
Mint. Christian VII. NGC Unc Details—Obverse Scratch. 
KM-257; Hede-14; Sieg-8; NMD-53; Schou-13a; Bruun-10836. 
Weight: 3.10 gms. Mintmaster Hans Jacob Arnold Branth. En -
graver: Anders Jonsen Lunde. Struck on a Danish Kurantdukat, 
1761 - 1763 by Hans Schivern Knoph (Fr-269; Hede-22E). The 
scratches in the obverse left field are presumably evidence of a 
test to decide if the coin was just a gilt silver 8 Skilling. Otherwise 
in spectacular condition, excessively rare and highly interesting. 
€5,000-€7,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.

Session II • Thursday , October 30, 2025 • 2:00  PM CET / 6:00  AM PT
COINS OF SWEDEN
Very Rare Type with Fully Legible Legends
12141
SWEDEN. Penny, ND (1022-50). Sigtuna Mint. Anund Jakob. NGC AU Details—Peck Marked. Llt-7; Delzanno-104; 
Malmer-14.310.1252; SMH-612; Bruun-11151. Weight: 1.43 gms. Moneyer: Thormodt. An old, dark toning covers the 
coin and boosts the already clear and well-struck devices. A very rare type with fully legible legends. The coin imitates 
Cnut’s Pointed Helmet coinage which is conventionally dated to the years between 1024 and 1030.
Of the two almost identical reverse dies from the moneyer Thormoth, Malmer knew of 28 coins (14 of each), with eight in 
private collections.
Son of Olof Skötkonung, the name ‘ Anund’ was given the king by the people, as his Christian name ‘Jakob’ was both too 
foreign and too strange sounding to them.
€5,000-€6,000
From the L.E. Bruun Collection.
Ex: Countship of Brahesminde Collection (Private Sale - 1922).

12142

no-47; Frösell-cf.19; Bruun-11156. Weight: 0.21 gms. Dissolved 
image of a hand holding a crozier. Very rare medieval type and a 
most splendid example in exceptional condition.
Presumable struck at the end of the reign of Knut Eriksson by the 
Archbishop of Uppsala.

harshly cleaned but with little circulation wear.

Hans (or Johan II as he known in Sweden) ruled the Kalmar Union 
of Denmark, Norway, and Sweden.

Excessively Rare First-Y ear Daler
12146
SWEDEN. Daler, 1534. Stockholm Mint. Gustav Vasa. NGC VF-30. Dav-8694; SM-109; Delzanno-10; Hagander-5; 
Bruun-11444. Weight: 28.66 gms. Mintmaster: Anders Hansson. An excessively rare type, this being the smaller por-
trait version with the king holding scepter and orb. Some double striking on both sides and golden toning, reverse with 
mottled patina.
An important piece of Swedish Numismatic history - 1534 being the first year, Dalers/Talers were struck in Sweden - and 
with an impressive provenance.
€25,000-€30,000

========== PAGE 232 ==========

Page 214 Stack’s Bowers Galleries
The L.  E. Bruun Col lection • A Corpus of Scandinavian Monetary History Part III
    
12147
SWEDEN. Salvator Daler, 1543. Svartsjö Mint. Gustav Vasa. NGC AU Details—Cleaned. Dav-8697; SM-163; Del-
zanno-19; Hagander-10; Bruun-11462. Weight: 28.88 gms. Mintmaster: Hans Hansson. Engraver: Ulrich von Nürnberg. 
Beautiful in every way with the handsome surfaces retaining lots of luster around the devices and pleasing toning in the 
peripheries. The mentioned cleaning has been carried out very, very carefully and is, while visible, almost not worth 
mentioning. The craftmanship on this emission is simply stunning and the detailing in the portraits of both the king and 
the Savior is exquisite. Lightly double-struck on the reverse and with a dark shadow to the left of the Savior, the coin still 
is nothing less than breathtaking.  
€1,500-€2,500
From the L.E. Bruun Collection.
Ex: Countship of Brahesminde Collection (Private Sale - 1922).
    
12148
SWEDEN. 1/2 Salvator Daler, 1544. Svartsjö Mint. Gustav Vasa. NGC EF-40. SM-171; Delzanno-32; Bruun-11469. 
Weight: 14.18 gms. Mintmaster: Hans Hansson. Engraver: Ulrich von Nürnberg. With light, even wear and a soft toning 
highlighting the devices, the eye-appeal of this scarce Half Taler cannot be ignored.
The number ‘194’ written in ink on the reverse refers to ‘Madai’s Vollständiges Thaler-Cabinet’ from 1767, which was used as 
a reference work throughout the 19th century. 
€3,000-€3,600
From the L.E. Bruun Collection.
Ex: Countship of Brahesminde Collection (Private Sale - 1922).

Bruun-11503. Mintmaster: Jost Höijer. Very rare type with little to no wear and handsomely repatinated.
The denomination can be found on the obverse in the exergue in Roman numerals.

========== PAGE 233 ==========

 Bid Online at www.StacksBowers.com Pa ge 215
Session II • Thursday , October 30, 2025 • 2:00  PM CET / 6:00  AM PT
Very Rare and Handsomely Toned Taler
12150
SWEDEN. Daler, 1594. Stockholm Mint. Sigismund. NGC VF-20. Dav-8709; SM-1a; Delzanno-1; Bruun-11651. 
Weight: 28.58 gms. Mintmaster: Gillis (Julius) Coyet, the Elder. Engraver: Henrik Isersnidare. Very rare. Worn on the 
high points, the obverse with eye-appeal well above the grade; the reverse less worn but with a more uneven toning.
Sigismund III Vasa was the son of Johan III and grandson of Gustav Vasa. Upon the death of his father, he had already 
inherited the throne in Catholic Poland from his maternal grandfather. The Swedish Nobility was not keen on having a 
Catholic king, but with the Uppsala Resolution of 1594, which secured Protestantism throughout the country, he was al-
lowed to take the Swedish throne. A devout Catholic, Sigismund blatantly disregarded the Resolution and civil war broke 
out, with his uncle, Karl (IX) on the side of the protestants. Sigismund lost and was officially deposed in 1599. While no 
longer king of Sweden, he remained king of Poland and fought many wars till his death in 1632 - same year as his cousin, 
Gustav II Adolf of Sweden. 
€15,000-€18,000
From the L.E. Bruun Collection.
Ex: Countship of Brahesminde Collection (Private Sale - 1922).
Ex: Ole Devegge Collection (Copenhagen - 1851) Lot # 5734.

making this a one-year type for mint.

========== PAGE 235 ==========

 Bid Online at www.StacksBowers.com Pa ge 217
Session II • Thursday , October 30, 2025 • 2:00  PM CET / 6:00  AM PT
Extremely Rare and in Superb Condition
12155
SWEDEN. 16 Mark, 1607. Stockholm Mint. Karl IX. NGC Unc Details—Bent. Fr-19; KM-25; SM-10; SG-11; Hagan-
der-53; Bruun-11703. Weight: 4.96 gms. Mintmaster: Antoni Grooth the Elder. Extremely rare and delightfully lustrous 
coin with a clear strike and a most excellent portrait. The very light bend can hardly be seen on the reverse and the 
overall condition makes this coin a contender for the best in private hands.
This fascinating piece of Swedish history was struck using gold collected as customs tax. From 1605, for every 100 Dalers 
worth of goods imported, 10 Rikdaler and 5 Hungarian Gulden were to be paid in tax and the gold collected used to mint 
Swedish coins such as this. The gold content was kept the same to facilitate the acceptance of the coins both in Sweden and 
abroad.
€20,000-€25,000
From the L.E. Bruun Collection.
Ex: Countship of Brahesminde Collection (Private Sale - 1922).
Ex: C. P . Humble Collection (Stockholm - 1852) Lot # 412 - sold for 18 Riksdaler, 25 Mark and 8 Skilling.

12156
SWEDEN. 8 Mark, 1608. Stockholm Mint. Karl IX. NGC AU Details—Cleaned. Dav-4512; KM-29; SM-37; Delzan-
no-25; Bruun-11711. Weight: 39.44 gms. Mintmaster: Antoni Grooth the Elder. Single-year issue in extraordinary condi-
tion for type with minimal surface wear and excellent details. A somewhat mottled, dark patina does little to conceal the 
fine features on this large denomination.
Finding an 8 Mark with as fine a strike and prominent pedigree as this example is not an easy task.

12158
SWEDEN. 1 Riksdaler, 1617. Söderköping Mint. Johan, Duke 
of Östergötland. NGC AU Details—Cleaned. Dav-4514; KM-
81.2; SM-1a; Delzanno-9; Hagander-63; Bruun-11743. Weight: 
31.37 gms. Mintmaster: David Kohl. Very rare two-year emis-
sion; hints of luster in the legend and only very light circulation 
wear with the reverse especially beautiful. 
A discreet ink number can be seen in obverse field, ‘2619’. The 
number refers to “Madai’s Vollsttändiges Thaler-Cabinet” from 
1767, which was used as a reference well into the 19th century.
Johan, prince of Östergötland, was the son of King Johan III, Half 
brother of King Sigismund, nephew of King Karl IX and cousin of 
King Gustav II Adolf.
€8,000-€10,000
From the L.E. Bruun Collection.
Ex: Countship of Brahesminde Collection (Private Sale - 1922).
    
12159
SWEDEN. 1/2 Mark, 1617. Stockholm Mint. Gustav II Adolf. 
NGC EF-40. KM-66; SM-76; Delzanno-76; Bruun-11763. Weigt: 
2.39 gms. Mintmaster: Gillis Gillisson Coyet, the Y ounger. Rare 
three-year emission with very distinct types for each year, this 
being the variation with small, laureate bust. This particular vari-
ation is the even rarer with the 'REFVG' spelling on the obverse. 
The coin has very pleasing surfaces and nice details throughout 
with a planchet flaw by the rim.
€1,400-€1,600
From the L.E. Bruun Collection.
Ex: Countship of Brahesminde Collection (Private Sale - 1922).
Ex: O. T. Thomsen Collection (Copenhagen - 1880) Lot # 4615.
    
12160
SWEDEN. 4 Mark, 1620. Stockholm Mint. Gustav II Adolf. 
NGC VF-25. KM-99,2; SM-62; Delzanno-59; Bruun-11774. 
Weight: 19.10 gms. Mintmaster: David kohl. Engraver: Hans 
Weiler. Excessively rare Small Bust variation. Weakly struck in 
central elements making the coin appear more worn than it is, as 
can be clearly seen in the legend - the Hebrew letters "Y ahweh/
Jehovah" above the king's head are still sharp.
Falcoin.se only registers one other Small Bust example, which is 
now at the Avesta Myntmuseum (Avesta Coin Museum).
€4,000-€5,000
From the L.E. Bruun Collection.
Ex: Countship of Brahesminde Collection (Private Sale - 1922).
    
12161
SWEDEN. Salvator Riksdaler, 1631. Sala Mint. Gustav II Ad-
olf. NGC EF Details—Cleaned. Dav-4520; KM-143; SM-36b; 
Delzanno-24; Hagander-cf. 81; Bruun-11850. Weight: 28.87 
gms. Mintmaster: Markus Kock. Very rare and excellently toned 
example; the concavity of the strike means the obverse appears 
worn but that is for the most part in the strike; the reverse is 
lustrous and beautiful.
The minting of silver coins stopped in 1626 in Stockholm and was 
resumed in Sala in 1631. In 1632 the mint moved back to Stock -
holm, but all silver coins dated 1631 are struck in Sala. 
€4,000-€6,000
From the L.E. Bruun Collection.
Ex: Countship of Brahesminde Collection (Private Sale - 1922).
Ex: Leopold Welzl von Wellenheim Collection (Vienna - 1846) Lot #

12162
SWEDEN. Augsburg. Ducat, 1632. Augsburg Mint. Gustav 
II Adolf. NGC AU Details—Bent. Fr-112f; KM-68; SB-3b; SG-
29; Hagander-89; Bruun-11871. Weight: 3.44 gms. Mintmaster: 
Johann Bartholomäus Hohleisen. Rare variation with horseshoe 
mintmark. Light waviness with no stress marks to the metal, 
hints of luster playing in the legend and a sharp strike with very 
little wear.
Augsburg in Southern Bavaria was conquered by Swedish forces in 
1632 and held till 1634, when the Imperial army retook it. Augs-
burg coins in the name of Gustav II Adolf were minted in 1632, 
1633, and 1634 (the two last dates posthumously). 
€2,000-€3,000
From the L.E. Bruun Collection.
Ex: Countship of Brahesminde Collection (Private Sale - 1922).
    
12163
SWEDEN. Erfurt. Ducat, 1634. Erfurt Mint. Gustav II Adolf. 
NGC AU-58. Fr-923; KM-54; SB15e; SG-53; Hagander-cf. 106; 
Bruun-11910. Weight: 3.44 gms. Mintmaster: Johann Schneider. 
Minute waviness to planchet; well-struck with a touch of softness 
in the very central parts of the reverse; a homogenous appear -
ance and hardly any wear but lots of eye-appeal.
These posthumous strikes (Gustav II Adolf died in 1632) have been 
subject to intense studies (Ottar Ertzheid Collection in particular). 
This specific variation does not appear to be included, though. It 
has two distinctly different flowers in the obverse legend above the 
portrait, and on the reverse the same two flowers are found, albeit 
superimposed. 
€1,500-€2,500
From the L.E. Bruun Collection.
Ex: Countship of Brahesminde Collection (Private Sale - 1922).
Ex: Leopold Welzl von Wellenheim Collection (Vienna - 1846) Lot # 
10108.
    
12164
SWEDEN. Stralsund. Taler, 1639-CS. Stralsund Mint. Kris-
tina. NGC AU-58. Dav-3831; KM-100; SB-6; Delzanno-396; 
not in Hagander; Bruun-11951. Weight: 28.42 gms. Mintmaster: 
Casper Sievers. Very handsome example of this interesting Taler. 
Smaller areas of softness but mostly a strong and clear strike - 
notice the feathers on the eagles and the central medallion on the 
reverse. Very rare in this high grade.
Having held minting right since the 13th century, Stralsund issued 
this Taler after the Swedish conquest of the city, bearing the arms of 
the city on the obverse (an arrow or spearhead) and the name and 
symbol of the Holy Roman Emperor on the reverse, but no mention 
of the Swedish rulers - much to the Swedes’ dismay. 
€2,500-€3,000
From the L.E. Bruun Collection.
Ex: Countship of Brahesminde Collection (Private Sale - 1922).
Gustav II Adolf by 
Jacob Hoefnagel (1573–1632/1633).

Extremely Rare and in Stunning Condition
12165
SWEDEN. 1/4 Riksdaler, 1641/0. Stockholm & Sala Mint. Kristina. NGC AU Details—Cleaned. KM-180; SM-35; 
Delzanno-59; Bruun-11963. Weight: 7.05 gms. Mintmaster: Marcus Kock. Engraver: Petter Michelsson. Excessively rare 
with less than 10 known. With no wear worth mentioning, lustrous surfaces with golden tones and only minor hairlines.
The last example sold in public auction was probably Bonde’s in 2007.
To get away from the plague-ridden city of Stockholm, the mint moved to Sala again in 1639. It moved back to Stockholm 
in 1641, and so for the coins of that date it is not yet possible to say in which mint they were produced. 
€3,000-€4,000
From the L.E. Bruun Collection.
Ex: Countship of Brahesminde Collection (Private Sale - 1922).

Reval, present day Tallinn, capital of Estonia, accepted Swedish 
dominion in 1561 to stave off the expanding Russian empire. The

SWEDEN. Swedish Livonia. Riga. Taler, 1660-IM. Riga Mint. Karl X Gustav. NGC AU-55. Dav-4596; KM-56; SB-92b; 
Hagander-297; Bruun-12138. Weight; 28.55 gms. Mintmaster: Jochim Meinecke. Very rare; variation with one flag on 
each tower. Much luster and wonderful golden and multicolor toning.
Riga was the second largest city in the Swedish Empire, surpassed only by the capital of Stockholm, and was held by the 
Swedes from the conquest by Gustav II Adolf (Gustavus Adolphus) in 1621 till the fall to the Russians during the plague of 
1710 (and The Great Northern War).

Very Rare Two-Y ear Type from Riga
Lustrous and Uncirculated
12175
SWEDEN. Swedish Livonia. Riga. 2 Ducat, 1667-IM. Riga Mint. Karl XI. NGC MS-61. Fr-17; KM-63; SB-87; SG-
131; Hagander-292; Bruun-12220. Weight: 6.88 gms. Mintmaster: Joachim Meinecke. Very rare two-year type, delight-
fully well-preserved, with much luster on the uncirculated, vibrantly golden surfaces. A light scratch is noted below the 
portrait which would explain why this very handsome example wasn’t given a higher grade.
For withstanding the Russian siege of 1656 and the bravery of its citizens in the war with Russia and Poland, Riga was 
awarded two crowns in their shield by the Swedish king; one on the lion in the portcullis and one above the crossed keys.
€20,000-€30,000
From the L.E. Bruun Collection.

Lustrous and Rare Ducat, Only 4484 Struck
12176
SWEDEN. Ducat, 1668. Stockholm Mint. Karl XI. NGC MS-61. Fr-42; KM-263; SM-14; SG-14; Hagander-235; Bru-
un-12227. Weight: 3.45 gms. Mintmaster: Abraham Kock Cronström. Engravers: Arvid Karlsteen and/or Johan Georg 
Breuer. Only 4484 struck. Very rare one-year type without drapes. Waviness to the planchet and some very light contact 
marks but retaining lots of playful luster and with remarkably sharp details; only a small striking softness in the very 
center on the reverse.
€5,000-€7,000
From the L.E. Bruun Collection.
Ex: Countship of Brahesminde Collection (Private Sale - 1922).
    
12177
SWEDEN. Bremen & Verden. 4 Mark, 1670/69-AH. Stade Mint. NGC AU-53. KM-79; SB-16; Delzanno-631; Bru-
un-12270. Weight: 22.13 gms. Mintmaster: Andreas Hille. A rare coin with only very light wear; somewhat rusty dies but 
fully worthy of its grade.
The Duchy of Bremen & Verden was occupied by the Swedish forces in 1632 and officially ceded to Sweden on the Westpha-
lian Peace of 1648. It remained Swedish till 1720, when it was again ceded to the Elector of Hannover, Georg (George I of 
Great Britain) at the end of the Great Nordic War.
The mint was placed in Stade as the City of Bremen was not part of the duchy and retained its own governing and minting 
rights.  
€1,200-€1,600
From the L.E. Bruun Collection.
Ex: Countship of Brahesminde Collection (Private Sale - 1922).

12181
SWEDEN. Stralsund. 2/3 Taler (Zweidrittel), 1678-HIH. 
Stralsund Mint. Karl XI. NGC EF-45. Dav-1009; KM-159; SB-
53; Delzanno-1059; Bruun-12363. Weight: 19.19 gms. Mintmas-
ter: Heinrich Johann Hille. Rare and superb condition for type. 
There are no real signs of circulation wear and only minor scuffs 
on the surface; the legend is bold and well-struck and the minor 
weaker points in the strike are only weak compared to the rest of 
the coin. The old toning on the coin seems to hide a hint of luster. 
A die flaw on the obverse by the denomination is a common trait 
for this date.
A number, ‘5129’, written in ink on the reverse, refers to ‘Madai’s 
Vollständiges Thaler-Cabinett’ from 1767, at the time and for may-
be a century the most complete catalogue of Taler coins. It was used 
as a reference work throughout the 19th century, and many of the 
coins in the L. E. Bruun Collection originating from the Ludwig de 
Traux Collection (Vienna - 1857) have a Madai reference number 
written on them.
€800-€1,000
From the L.E. Bruun Collection.
Ex: Countship of Brahesminde Collection (Private Sale - 1922).
Ex: Ludwig de Traux Collection (Vienna - 1857) Lot # 8827.

Szczecin (or Stettin) was the capitol of Swedish Pomerania from 
the Peace at Westphalia in 1648 till 1713 during which time the 
city was heavily fortified and also besieged several times.

12188
SWEDEN. Pomerania. Taler, 1709-IM. Szczecin Mint. Karl

but with handsome grey surfaces and luster; multicolor toning 
around the devices.
Before the Battle of Poltava in July 1709, which Karl XII famously 
lost, he had invaded Saxony to force Augustus II to renounce the 
Polish throne, which he did. Karl then restored the rights of reli -
gious freedom to the Protestants there. This Taler was struck com-
memorating these restored rights.

The Only Swedish Double Ducat from Kristina
12192
SWEDEN. 2 Ducat, 1719-LC. Stockholm Mint. Ulrika Eleonora. NGC VF-25. Fr-54; KM-372; SM-1; SG-1; Hagan-
der-341; Bruun-12726. Weight: 7.47 gms. Mintmaster: Lorentz Careelberg. Engraver: Johann Carl Hedlinger. Sought 
one-year type and the only Double Ducat from the reign of Ulrika Eleonora. The weakly struck portrait makes the coins 
appear more worn than it is. There is sparkle in the surfaces and sharp details.
The gold for the large Ducat emission 1719-1720 was purchased from France to be used in the war against Russia. The gold 
was substandard but this was compensated for by the extra weight.

========== PAGE 251 ==========

 Bid Online at www.StacksBowers.com Pa ge 233
Session II • Thursday , October 30, 2025 • 2:00  PM CET / 6:00  AM PT
PLATE MONEY OF SWEDEN
The one thing that really makes Swedish numismatics stand out 
is the Plate Money. Struck from 1644 till 1776, the large, chunky 
slabs of stamped, hammered copper have, in the eyes of the nu-
mismatic world, become synonymous with Swedish coinage.
I the mid-17th century the Swedish copper mines supplied up -
wards of 80% of the copper used in Europe. Heavily reliant on 
the revenue from the sale to finance its many wars, the idea was 
presented to the Swedish Privy Council to use some of the cop -
per to mint large denomination coins and keeping a portion of 
the copper off the international market, thus increasing the mar-
ket price.  
Since silver was worth more than 200 times that of copper, the 
plate coins were very large. The largest ever produced was the 10 
Daler, which weighs almost 20 kilos.  However, since all but one 
of those are in museums, the 8 Daler is considered the largest 
obtainable circulating coin in the world.
Almost all the plate coins were produced in Avesta, northeast of 
Stockholm and close to the copper mine of Falu. The production 
was simple and cheap: the copper was heated only once, hammered to an even thickness and then cut into size and 
weight. Five stamps were then placed on the plate – one in each corner with the date and the name of cipher of the 
ruler, and one at the centre with the denomination. 
Plate money was struck from 1644 till 1776 – these latest ones ante -
dated 1768 and produced exclusively for export purposes. In fact most 
of the plate money, that has survived to this day, comes from outside 
of Sweden, mainly from shipwrecks. Demonetized Plate money was 
popular as ballast which would be traded for colonial goods. The two 
best known finds of plate money are the Nicobar, which was wrecked 
in 1783 in False Bay, South Africa, and the Schimmelmann, which ran 
aground at Cape Verde in 1781. 
The Swedish scholar Dr. Bertel Tingström produced an impressive 
tome on plate money in 1986. The book not only describes the rea -
son for the emergence of the plate money, the historical and econom-
ic contexts, and produces a catalogue of types, dates and variations; 
Tingström also lists the number of plate money produced and the 
numbers surviving today, to the best of his knowledge. It is these num-
bers we refer to in the individual texts. Unfortunately, Tingström does 
not specify, how many surviving examples are in public collection and 
on private hands; the number still does, however, give a indication of 
the rarity.
Falun Copper Mine, painting by contemporary artist 
Per Hilleström (1732-1816).  
The Swedish National Museum, Stockholm
Swedish plate money was produced using copper
from Sweden's abundant mines.


========== PAGE 252 ==========

Page 234 Stack’s Bowers Galleries
The L.  E. Bruun Col lection • A Corpus of Scandinavian Monetary History Part III
The World’s Largest Obtainable, Circulating Coin
Extremely Rare in Private Ownership
    
 
 
Photo Reduced
12197
SWEDEN. 8 Daler Plate Money, 1659. Avesta Mint. Karl X Gustav. KM-PM13; SM-47; Delzanno-58; Tingström-Plate 
p. 254, stamps B/4; Bruun-13813. Dimensions: 625 x 131 mm. Weight: 14.530 kg. Excellent, even example with no corro-
sion, an agreeable color and with all five stamps clearly struck.
With only one example of the 10 Daler 1644 in private hands, the famous 8 Daler is not only the largest collectable plate 
money but actually the largest circulating coin the world has ever seen and has for that reason always ranked among the 
most coveted Scandinavian coin of all. Tingström knew of 24 examples with most being in public collections around the 
world.
€150,000-€200,000
From the L.E. Bruun Collection.

In 1718 a depreciation of the plate coinage took place, making their new value 2/3 of the old. The coins in the possession of the 
Crown or the banks were exempt, however, and were fitted with the countermark of the arms of Götaland.

COINS OF GERMANY
Spectacular 2.5 Broad Taler

12204
GERMANY . Schleswig-Holstein-Gottorp. 2 1/2 Talers, ND (1607-16). Johann Adolf. NGC AU-55. Dav-unlisted, cf. 
LS444; KM-unlisted, cf. 3; Sieg-12.4; Lange-cf. 264D; Bruun-14147. Weight: 73.07 gms. Excessively rare, unlisted as 2.5 
Taler in KM, Lange and Davenport. This spectacular and impressive 2.5 Taler is only listed in Sieg, who were aware of 
the L. E. Bruun Collection. The closest we can come to anything like this coin is a 1.5 Taler from the same dies, which 
was sold in 2021. The present coin has wonderful, original surfaces and plenty of luster.
€10,000-€15,000
From the L.E. Bruun Collection.
Ex: Countship of Brahesminde Collection (Private Sale - 1922).
 Ex: Christian Jürgensen Thomsen Collection (Copenhagen - 1876) Lot # 2474.
CHRISTIAN JURGENSEN THOMSEN  
(1788-1865)  
Christian Jurgensen Thomsen (1788-1865) was an internation -
ally famous antiquarian, numismatist and director of the Nation-
al Museum of Denmark. He is considered the originator of the 
three-age system of European antiquity, dividing pre-history into 
the Stone, Bronze and Iron Ages. Thomsen was also a prominent 
numismatist, and in many ways his collection of European medi-
eval coins has never been surpassed.


========== PAGE 260 ==========

Page 242 Stack’s Bowers Galleries
The L.  E. Bruun Col lection • A Corpus of Scandinavian Monetary History Part III
Extremely Rare Single-Y ear Taler
12205
GERMANY . Schleswig-Holstein-Gottorp. Taler, 1674-AH. Stade Mint. Christian Albrecht. NGC Unc Details—
Cleaned. Dav-3704; KM-120; Sieg-7.1; Lange-375; Bruun-14419. Weight: 28.88 gms. Mintmaster: Andreas Hille. Ex-
tremely rare single-year Taler issue. Lustrous with some smoothing in obverse fields; the reverse uncirculated with 
beautiful, subtle toning; exquisite detailing throughout. Only one other example is listed as sold (twice) on CoinAr -
chives, this present example being in far better condition.
The shield on the reverse holds the arms of Norway, Schleswig, Holstein, Stormarn, and Ditmarschen, with the arms of 
Oldenburg-Delmenhorst in the center. 
€14,000-€18,000
From the L.E. Bruun Collection.

12209
GERMANY . Schleswig-Holstein-Gottorp. 2 Mark, ND (1681-
82). Schleswig Mint. Christian Albrecht. NGC EF-40. Dav-
unlisted (cf. 577); KM-unlisted (cf. 136); Sieg-16.1; Lange-399C; 
Bruun-14473. Weight: 22.00 gms. Mintmaster: Claus Jakob 
Mecklenburg. Excessive rare ND issue with the Duke’s Motto 
‘Per Aspera Ad Astra’ on the reverse instead of denomination 
and date. Excellent surfaces with old toning.
The famous Christian Lange Collection only had a rubbing of the 
example in the Danish National Museum and knew of one more 
example, sold in Copenhagen in 1871 as Lot #2612 at C. J. Thom-
sen’s auction. That Coin was bought by the Hamburg merchant 
and coin collector Mayntzhusen, who sold his entire collection 
to the Danish National Museum. As this coin was already in the 
museum collection, they in turn sold it in auction in 1883 along 
with other duplicates. Here it was bought by the Count Bille-Brahe, 
whose collection was purchased en bloc by L. E. Bruun in 1922. It 
is this coin being offered here. 
€3,000-€3,600
From the L.E. Bruun Collection.

in 1883.

only had a rubbing of the example in the Danish National Museum.
€6,000-€8,000
From the L.E. Bruun Collection.
Ex: Countship of Brahesminde Collection (Private Sale - 1922).
Ex: H. M. Mayntzhusen Collection, purchased en bloc by the Royal Danish Coins and Medals Collection and the duplicates sold 
at auction in 1883.

========== PAGE 263 ==========

 Bid Online at www.StacksBowers.com Pa ge 245
Session II • Thursday , October 30, 2025 • 2:00  PM CET / 6:00  AM PT
    
12211
GERMANY . Schleswig-Holstein-Gottorp. Mark, 1698. Tön -
ning Mint. Friedrich IV . NGC EF-45. KM-165; Sieg-6; Lange-
430A; Bruun-14495. Weight: 10.84 gms. Mintmaster: Hans 
Heinrich Luders. Very rare single-year emission. Excellent de-
tails with a slight weakness as often seen on the coins from that 
mint, and an old, uneven toning.
Mark emissions from Schleswig-Holstein are rare, this being the 
second of only two. 
€2,000-€2,400
From the L.E. Bruun Collection.
Ex: Countship of Brahesminde Collection (Private Sale - 1922).
    
12212
GERMANY . Schleswig-Holstein-Sonderburg. Taler, 
1627//1626. Reinfeldt or Sonderburg Mint. Alexander. NGC 
VF Details—Cleaned. Dav-3717; KM-28; Sieg-5; Lange-560; 
Bruun-14678. Weight: 27.26 gms. Mintmaster: Johann Lilien-
thal. Very rare Taler struck on the death of Alexander in 1627. 
Unevenly toned but gleaming with multicolor. An interesting 
zwitter/mule in that the reverse die of the 1626 Taler was reused 
on this coin, giving it two different dates.
The Norwegian lion sits atop the reverse arms as the dukes bore the 
hereditary title of ‘heir to Norway’ or ‘Heres Norvegie’. 
€3,000-€4,000
From the L.E. Bruun Collection.
Ex: Countship of Brahesminde Collection (Private Sale - 1922).
    
12213
GERMANY . Schleswig-Holstein-Schaumburg-Pinneberg. 
Taler, 1589. Altona Mint. Adolf XIII. NGC AU-50. Dav-8239; 
Lange-790; Weinmeister-30/31; Bruun-14788. Weight: 28.96 
gms. Mintmaster: Georg Michaelsen. A rare and alluring coin 
with a lovely rainbow toning. 
€1,500-€2,000
From the L.E. Bruun Collection.
Ex: Countship of Brahesminde Collection (Private Sale - 1922).
Ex: H. M. Mayntzhusen Collection, purchased en bloc by the Royal 
Danish Coins and Medals Collection and the duplicates sold at auction 
in 1883.
    
12214
GERMANY . Schleswig-Holstein-Schaumburg-Pinneberg. 
Taler, 1590. Altona Mint. Adolf XIII. NGC AU-50. Dav-8241; 
Lange-unlisted; Weinmeister-32; Bruun-14793. Weight: 28.85 
gms. Mintmaster: Abraham Symons. Extremely rare Taler; deep, 
original toning with rainbow coloring scattered over the excep-
tionally well-preserved surfaces. 
€2,000-€3,000
From the L.E. Bruun Collection.
Ex: Countship of Brahesminde Collection (Private Sale - 1922).
Ex: H. M. Mayntzhausen Collection, purchased en bloc by the Royal 
Danish Coins and Medals Collection and the duplicates sold at auction 
in 1883.

Page 246 Stack’s Bowers Galleries
The L.  E. Bruun Col lection • A Corpus of Scandinavian Monetary History Part III
Extremely Rare and Lustrous 1.5 Broad Taler
    
12215
GERMANY . Schleswig-Holstein-Schaumburg-Pinneberg. 1 1/2 Taler, 1598. Altona Mint. Adolf XIII. NGC AU-58. 
Dav-Ls456; Weinmeister-29; Lange-cf. 788; Bruun-14815. Weight: 43.17 gms. Extremely rare Broad Taler, lustrous and 
with a fine toning starting from the peripheries in. The touches of flatness in the high point stem from the strike. A 
superb piece for any collection.
€10,000-€12,000
From the L.E. Bruun Collection.
Ex: Countship of Brahesminde Collection (Private Sale - 1922).
    
12216
GERMANY . Schleswig-Holstein-Schaumburg-Pinneberg. Ta-
ler, 1615. Altona Mint. Ernst III. NGC Unc Details—Cleaned. 
Dav-3738; KM-74; Weinmeister-130; Bruun-14871. Weight: 
28.81 gms. Mintmaster: Henning Hanses. An extraordinary and 
very rare survivor; lightly cleaned as seen in the fields and the 
portrait, the coin has been repatinating for over a century and 
a stunning, metallic-blue color is spreading from the periphery 
on both sides. The reverse seems practically untouched and the 
charming shield topped with helmets stands razor-sharp. 
€1,000-€1,200
From the L.E. Bruun Collection.
Ex: Countship of Brahesminde Collection (Private Sale - 1922).
    
12217
GERMANY . Schleswig-Holstein-Schaumburg-Pinneberg. Ta-
ler, 1620. Altona Mint. Ernst III. NGC AU-58. Dav-3739; KM-
106; Weinmeister-; Lange-; Bruun-14891. Weight: 28.82 gms. 
Mintmaster: Christoph Feistell. Excessively rare Taler in sublime 
condition for type. The obverse is almost uncirculated as can be 
seen on the ears and hair especially, and with a fine gray toning 
with golden highlights in the legend; the reverse has a slightly 
uneven yet still very appealing toning, the detailing in the shield 
and helmets simply breathtaking set against the lustrous back -
ground.
With only one recorded sale on CoinArchives, damaged and dug 
up, and the type also missing from the famous Lange Collection, 
the sale of this marvelous piece is an opportunity not to be missed. 
€2,000-€2,400
From the L.E. Bruun Collection.
Ex: Countship of Brahesminde Collection (Private Sale - 1922).

Session II • Thursday , October 30, 2025 • 2:00  PM CET / 6:00  AM PT
    
12218
GERMANY . Schleswig-Holstein-Schaumburg-Pinneberg. Ta-
ler, 1622. Altona Mint. Ernst III. NGC AU-55. Dav-3746var; 
KM-130; Lange-Cf 944a; Weinmaster-153; Bruun-14906. 
Weight: 28.93 gms. Mintmaster: Thomas Eisenbein. Struck on 
the death of Ernst III on the 17th of January 1622. Highly attrac-
tive Death Taler with a lovely rainbow toning and sharp details. 
Variation with the obverse of Dav-3746 and an unlisted reverse. 
€1,500-€2,000
From the L.E. Bruun Collection.
Ex: Countship of Brahesminde Collection (Private Sale - 1922).
    
12219
GERMANY . Schleswig-Holstein-Schaumburg-Pinneberg. Ta-
ler, 1622. Altona Mint. Ernst III. NGC VF-35. Dav-3746a; KM-
130; Lange-Cf 944a; Weinmeister-cf. 153; Bruun-14907. Weight: 
28.46 gms. Mintmaster: Thomas Eisenbein. Rare Death Taler; 
very weak in the most central parts and some double striking 
in the obverse legend but the coin seems far better than the VF 
grade, and a dark, old toning with hints of blue accentuates the 
devices for much eye-appeal. 
€1,800-€2,200
From the L.E. Bruun Collection.
Ex: Countship of Brahesminde Collection (Private Sale - 1922).
    
12220
GERMANY . Schleswig-Holstein-Schaumburg-Pinneberg. 
1/2 Taler, ND (1601-22). Altona Mint. Ernst III. NGC AU-53. 
KM-102; Lange cf. 920; Weinmeister-159; Bruun-14918. Weight: 
14.27 gms. Extremely rare Half Taler in extraordinary condition. 
An old, dark patina covers the surfaces and gives the coin, along 
with the colorful toning, a most sublime expression. Light wear 
to the high points but well-struck and with excellent details.
Portrait coins from Ernst III are all very rare; the sublime portrait 
and spectacular condition of this coin makes it a true unicorn. 
€1,000-€1,400
From the L.E. Bruun Collection.
Ex: Countship of Brahesminde Collection (Private Sale - 1922).
Ex: H. M. Mayntzhusen Collection (Copenhagen - 1883) Lot # 171.
    
12221

Taler, 1628. Altona Mint. Justus Herman (Jobst Hermann). 
NGC AU-53. Dav-3753; KM-143; Lange-954; Weinmeister-336; 
Bruun-14953. Weight: 28.89 gms. An immensely attractive coin 
with rich colorful toning. 
€1,000-€1,500
From the L.E. Bruun Collection.

Page 248 Stack’s Bowers Galleries
The L.  E. Bruun Col lection • A Corpus of Scandinavian Monetary History Part III
    
12222
GERMANY . Lübeck (Bishopric). Taler, 1612. Bremervörde 
Mint. Johann Friedrich. NGC EF-40. Dav-5073; KM-19.1; 
Lange-cf. 492C; Bruun-14975. Weight: 28.49 gms. Extremely 
rare date and type. Lovely, dark toning with hints of gold and 
multicolor and only slight wear to the high points. Some marks 
in the obverse fields. 
€2,000-€3,000
From the L.E. Bruun Collection.
Ex: Countship of Brahesminde Collection (Private Sale - 1922).
Ex: Ole Devegge Collection (Copenhagen - 1851, 1866, 1867) Lot # 
5548.
    
12223
GERMANY . Lübeck (Bishopric). 2 Mark, 1723-AW . Glück -
stadt Mint. Christian August. NGC AU-53. Dav-623; KM-78.1; 
Lange-511A; Bruun-15039. Weight: 17.43 gms. Mintmaster: An-
dreas Woltereck. A very rare 2 Mark or Gulden as seen by the 
lack of a photo or even a drawing of it in Davenport. The surfaces 
are uncleaned with a greenish tint hiding promises of luster, and 
while there is a softness to the top points in the portrait it seems 
to be a weakness in the strike rather than wear or possibly a com-
bination.
Christian August of Holstein-Gottorp-Eutin was the grandson on 
his mother’s side of King Frederik III of Denmark and father to 
King Adolf Fredrik of Sweden. He was a knight of the Danish Order 
of the Elephant; the order can be seen under the portrait. 
No recorded sales on CoinArchives for either type of the 1723 2 
Mark speaks to their rarity. 
€1,400-€1,800
From the L.E. Bruun Collection.
Ex: Countship of Brahesminde Collection (Private Sale - 1922).
    
12224
GERMANY . Lübeck (Bishopric). 2 Mark, 1723-AW . Glück -
stadt Mint. Christian August. NGC MS-62. Dav-623; KM-78.2; 
Lange-cf. 511A; Bruun-15040. Weight: 17.10 gms. Mintmaster: 
Andreas Woltereck. A very rare 2 Mark or Gulden in extraordi-
nary condition; old, greenish tinted toning covers the uncircu -
lated surfaces with luster shimmering underneath. A die crack 
runs through the portrait. The elegant details in the hair shows 
off not only the artistry of the die cutter but also the condition of 
the piece, as there are no flattened part, neither from weak strike 
nor wear.
No recorded sales on Coinarchives for either type of the 1723 2 
Mark speaks to their rarity. 
€2,000-€3,000
From the L.E. Bruun Collection.
Ex: Countship of Brahesminde Collection (Private Sale - 1922).
Ex: Leopold Welzl von Wellenheim Collection (Vienna - 1846) Lot # 
5144.
    
12225
GERMANY . Rantzau. 2/3 Taler (Zweidrittel), 1689. Rantzau 
Mint. Detlef. NGC MS-62. Dav-778; KM-4; Lange-1011a; Bru-
un-15065. Weight: 17.22 gms. Rare Zweidrittel in immaculate 
condition. Finding a Mint State example of this type is exces -
sively rare, and judging from the other examples, that have sold 
publicly in the last decades, this coin is Non Plus Ultra. A light 
golden toning adorns the shimmering surfaces further enhanc -
ing the eye-appeal. 
€1,500-€2,000
From the L.E. Bruun Collection.
Ex: Countship of Brahesminde Collection (Private Sale - 1922).
Ex: H. M. Mayntzhusen Collection, purchased en bloc by the Royal 
Danish Coins and Medals Collection and the duplicates sold at auction 
in 1883.

12227
GERMANY . Oldenburg. 1/4 Taler, 1667. Jever Mint. Anton Günther. NGC AU-50. KM-91; Trippler/Kalvelage-309; 
Bruun-15103. Weight: 7.21 gms. Mintmaster: David Ziegenhorn. Rare Quarter Death Taler; Wonderfully patinated, 
nearly uncirculated and with an incredible provenance. 
€800-€1,000
From the L.E. Bruun Collection.
Ex: Countship of Brahesminde Collection (Private Sale - 1922).
Ex: Leopold Welzl von Wellenheim Collection (Vienna - 1846) Lot # 4942.
    
12228
GERMANY . Oldenburg. Lübeck (Bishopric). Taler, 1775. Altona Mint. Friedrich August. NGC AU-58. Dav-2411; 
KM-85; Trippler/Kalvelage-371; Lange 523; Bruun-15140. Weight: 28.89 gms. Mintmaster: Caspar Heinrich Lyng. Excel-
lent condition for type with playful luster around the devices and attractive, old toning; only 1,000 pieces struck.
Struck to Danish standard in 1777 re-using the reverse of the Albertstaler of 1775, the obverse bears the title ‘DVX REGN 
OLD’ - ‘Duke Regent of Oldenburg’, a title he was only granted in 1776.
Friedrich August only had three coins struck: the 1776 gold Pistole, the 1775 Albertstaler and this, the 1775 (1777) Spec -
iestaler. All coins are considered presentation pieces and were struck in low numbers. 
€1,400-€1,600
From the L.E. Bruun Collection.
Ex: Countship of Brahesminde Collection (Private Sale - 1922).
End of Session II

Trippler/Kalvelage – Hartmus Trippler & Heinrich Kalvelage. Münzen der Grafen, Herzöge und Grossherzöge von Oldenburg. 
Osnabrück. 1996.
Weinmeister – Paul Weinmeister. Münzgeschichteder Grafschaft Holstein-Schauenburg. Zeitschrift für Numismatik 26. Berlin. 1908.

========== PAGE 278 ==========

TERMS AND CONDITIONS OF SALE
1. Auction Basics
This is a public auction sale (“ Auction Sale”) conducted by 
auctioneer, Stack’s Bowers Galleries (hereinafter referred 
to as “ Auctioneer” and at times as “Stack’s Bowers”). Bid-
ding in this Auction Sale constitutes acceptance by you 
(“Bidder”) of all the Terms and Conditions of Sale stated 
herein (the “Terms of Sale”). Bidders may include con-
signors who may bid and purchase lots in the Auction 
Sale consigned by the consignor pursuant to their con -
signment agreement with Stack’s Bowers (“Consignor” or 
“Consignors”). A Consignor that bids on their own lots in 
the Auction Sale may pay a different fee than the Buyer’s 
Premium charged to all other Buyers. Stack’s Bowers re-
serves the right to include in any auction sale its own ma-
terial as well as material from affiliated or related compa-
nies, principals, officers or employees. Stack’s Bowers may 
have direct or indirect interests in any of the lots in the 
auction and may collect commissions. THE TWO PRE -
CEDING SENTENCES SHALL BE DEEMED A PART 
OF THE DESCRIPTION OF ALL LOTS CONTAINED 
IN THE CATALOG. Where the Consignor has repur -
chased a lot and the lot is either returned to the Consign-
or or otherwise dealt with or disposed of in accordance 
with the Consignor’s direction, or pursuant to contractual 
agreement, Stack’s Bowers reserves the right to so note in 
the prices realized or to omit a price from the prices real-
ized. Lots may carry a reserve (“Reserve”). A Reserve is 
a price or bid below which the Auctioneer will not sell 
an item or will repurchase an item on behalf of the Con -
signor or for Stack’s Bowers. Reserves may be confidential 
and not disclosed. The Buyer is the Bidder who makes the 
highest bid accepted by the Auctioneer, and includes the 
principal of any Bidder acting as an agent. Auctioneer re-
serves the right to cancel or postpone the Auction Sale 
or any session thereof for any reason whatsoever, and no 
Bidder shall have any claim as a result of any such cancel-
lation or postponement.
2. Descriptions and Grading
Bidder acknowledges that grading of most coins, cur -
rency and cryptocurrency tokens in this Auction Sale 
have been determined by independent grading services, 
and those that are not graded by an independent grad -
ing service may be graded by Stack’s Bowers. Grading of 
rare coins, currency and cryptocurrency tokens is subjec-
tive and, even though grading has a material effect on the 
value of the coins, currency and cryptocurrency tokens, 
grading may differ among independent grading services 
and among numismatists. Stack’s Bowers is not respon -
sible for the grades assigned by independent grading ser-
vices, and makes no warranty or representation regarding 
such grades. Bidder further acknowledges and agrees that 
grades assigned by Stack’s Bowers and lot descriptions 
are based solely upon an examination of the coins, cur -
rency and cryptocurrency tokens and are intended solely 
to identify coins, currency and cryptocurrency and note 
any perceived characteristics. However, coin grading and 
descriptions by Stack’s Bowers are subjective. Stack’s Bow-
ers does not warrant the accuracy of such grading or de -
scriptions, nor do they in any way form the basis for any 
bid. Unless noted, all photographs in this catalog are of 
the actual items being sold but may not be the actual size 
or scale.
3. The Bidding Process
The Auctioneer shall have the right to open or accept bid-
ding on any lot by placing a bid on behalf of: the Con -
signor or his or her agent; a mail, telephone, Internet or 
telefax Bidder; or any other participant in the Auction 
Sale. Bids must be for an entire lot and each lot consti -
tutes a separate sale. All bids must be on an increment or 
half increment  (a cut bid) as established by the Auction-
eer. Non-conforming bids will be rounded down to the 
nearest half or full increment and this rounded bid will 
be the Bidder’s high bid. No lot will be broken up unless 
otherwise permitted by the Auctioneer. Lots will be sold 
in their numbered sequence unless Auctioneer directs 
otherwise. All material shall be sold in separate lots to 
the highest Bidder as determined by the Auctioneer. Auc-
tioneer shall have the right in its sole and absolute discre-
tion to accept or decline any bid, establish bid increments, 
challenge any bid or bidding increment, to reduce any 
mail bid received, adjudicate all bidding disputes, to ex -
clude any Bidder and to determine the prevailing bid. The 
Auctioneer shall have the right, but not the obligation, to 
rescind the acceptance of any bid and place the lot(s) for 
Auction Sale again. Auctioneer’s decision on all bidding 
disputes shall be binding and final. For Bidder’s protec -
tion, no “unlimited” or “buy” bids will be accepted. When 
identical bids are received for a lot, preference is given to 
the first bid received as determined by the Auctioneer. For 
example, a mail bid will take precedence over an identical 
floor bid or live internet bid. Cut bids are only accepted on 
bids greater than €500 and each Bidder may only execute 
one cut bid per lot. All bids are final and cannot be can -
celled or withdrawn without the express consent of the 
Auctioneer, in its sole discretion. Bids will not be accepted 
from persons under eighteen (18) years of age without a 
parent’s written consent which acknowledges the Terms 
of Sale herein and agrees to be bound thereby on behalf of 
the underage Bidder. The Auction Sale is complete when 
the Auctioneer so announces by the fall of the hammer or 
in any other customary manner.
THIS IS NOT AN APPROV AL SALE. Except as oth -
erwise expressly provided in these Terms of Sale, NO 
PURCHASED ITEMS MAY BE RETURNED FOR ANY 
REASON. All prospective Bidders who examine the lot(s) 
prior to the Auction Sale personally assume all respon -
sibility for any damage that Bidder causes to the lot(s). 
Stack’s Bowers shall have sole reasonable discretion in 
determining the value of the damage caused by a Bidder, 
which shall be promptly paid by such Bidder.
STACK’S BOWERS IS NOT RESPONSIBLE FOR ANY 
ERRORS IN BIDDING. All Bidders should make certain 
to bid on the correct lot and that the bid is the bid intend-
ed. Once the hammer has fallen and the Auctioneer has 
announced the Buyer, the Buyer is unconditionally bound 
to pay for the lot, even if the Buyer made a mistake. Stack’s 
Bowers reserves the right to withdraw any lot at any time. 
No participant in the Auction Sale shall have a right to 
claim any damages, including consequential damages if a 
lot is withdrawn, even if the withdrawal occurs after the 
Auction Sale.
Page 260 Stack’s Bowers Galleries

========== PAGE 279 ==========

TERMS AND CONDITIONS OF SALE
4. Bidder Registration Required
All persons seeking to bid must complete and sign a reg -
istration card either at the auction or online, or otherwise 
qualify to bid, as determined in the sole discretion of the 
Auctioneer. By submitting a bid, the Bidder acknowledges 
that Bidder has read the Terms of Sale, the descriptions 
for the lot(s) on which they have bid, and that they agree 
to be bound by these Terms of Sale. The Bidder acknowl-
edges that the invoice describing a lot by number incor -
porates the catalog and Terms of Sale. Person appearing 
on the U.S.A. ’s Office of Foreign Assets Control (“OFAC”) 
list, or any comparable list issued by the country hosting 
the auction are not eligible to bid.
5. Buyer’s Premiums and Value Added Tax (V AT)
For Buyers who take delivery of lots in non-EU coun-
tries, a premium of twenty percent (20%) based upon the 
total amount of the hammer (minimum of €25), will be 
added to all purchases of individual lots (the “Buyer’s Pre-
mium”). Danish V AT will not apply, provided that the lots 
in question are exported outside of the EU within 30 days 
from the date of the Auction Sale. 
Buyers being non-V AT registered entities or individuals 
who take delivery of lots within EU countries subject to 
margin scheme taxation (in Danish “brugtmoms”) will be 
invoiced a V AT charge and a Buyer’s Premium for a total 
of twenty five percent (25%) based upon the total amount 
of the hammer (minimum of €25), which will be added 
to all purchases of individual lots. For Buyers who take 
delivery of lots within EU countries which are either V AT-
exempt or subject to regular V AT, a Buyer’s Premium of 
twenty percent (20%) based upon the total amount of the 
hammer (minimum of €25), will be added (excluding 
Danish V AT on the hammer plus Buyer’s premium). Lots 
subject to regular V AT will be marked on the website and 
in the catalog with the symbol “u” or with the text “This 
item is subject to full V AT” .
Institutional EU Buyers with a valid V AT number regis-
tered in an EU member state (other than Denmark) will 
be charged a Buyer’s Premium, but may be exempt from 
Danish V AT if said EU Buyer provides the requisite statu-
tory registration documents and can provide documenta-
tion that the lot has been transported to the foreign ad -
dress (i.e. outside Denmark) provided by the EU Buyer 
to the Auctioneer. These documents comprise the V AT 
registration number and the acknowledgement of receipt 
from the territory of the EU member state in which the 
V AT registration was issued. Should the Auctioneer effect 
the export of any lots, and provided a valid V AT registra-
tion document was submitted to the Auctioneer before 
purchase, V AT will not be charged.
Buyer’s Premiums as described above will be applied re -
gardless of affiliation with any group or organization.  A 
reacquisition charge may apply to Consignors pursuant to 
a separate agreement, which may be higher or lower than 
the Buyer’s Premium.
Any import (and/or turnover) tax and duty payable 
abroad is on Buyer’s account in every case. 
6. Payment
Payment is due immediately upon the fall of the Auction-
eer’s hammer. Payment is delinquent and in default if not 
received in full, in good funds, within fourteen (14) calen-
dar days of the Auction Sale (the “Default Date”), without 
exception, time being of the essence. All invoices will be 
made in Euros. Unless otherwise agreed in writing prior 
to the Auction Sale, all auction sales are payable strictly in 
U.S. Dollars or Euros. If payment is made in U.S Dollars, 
Buyer’s invoices will be credited with the amount of Euros 
at the currency exchange rate established by the Auction-
eer on the date of the  of the Auction Sale. Provided, this 
exchange rate is only valid for payments made on the date 
of the Auction Sale and will thereafter fluctuate. Payments 
made in U.S. Dollars after the date of the Auction Sale 
will be credited with the lower of the currency exchange 
rate on the payment date or the date of the Auction Sale. 
Buyer will be responsible for payment of any shortfall 
upon receipt of notice from the Auctioneer of such short-
fall amount. Payments may be made by U.S. Dollars or 
Euros, checks from U.S. banks, wire transfer or cashier’s 
check. Cash transactions will not be accepted. Contact the 
Auctioneer for wiring instructions before sending a wire. 
Wire transfers from banks located outside of the United 
States are subject to an international bank wire fee of €35. 
Payment by ACH/eCheck will be accepted upon prior 
approval by Auctioneer. Payment by credit card (Visa, 
Mastercard, American Express and Discover) or Paypal 
will be accepted upon prior approval by Auctioneer. All 
payments by credit card or Paypal will incur a surcharge 
of 2.5%. This fee only applies to credit card or Paypal 
transactions, and does not exceed Auctioneer’s cost of 
processing these payments. Payment by check, ACH/
eCheck, wire transfer or cashier’s check will not incur this 
surcharge. All payments are subject to a clearing period 
determined by the Auctioneer. Stack’s Bowers reserves the 
right not to release lots for which good funds have not yet 
been received in full. On any past due accounts, Stack’s 
Bowers reserves the right, without notice, to extend credit 
and impose carrying charges (as described below). Buyers 
agree to pay reasonable attorney’s fees and costs incurred 
to collect past due accounts as permitted under applicable 
law. Any invoice not paid by the Default Date will bear 
interest at the highest permissible in accordance with 
applicable law.  . Buyers personally and unconditionally 
guarantee payment in full of all amounts owed to Stack’s 
Bowers. Any person by submitting bids on behalf of a cor-
poration or other entity, by making such bid, agrees to be 
personally jointly and severally liable for the payment of 
the purchase price and any related charges and the perfor-
mance of all Buyer obligations under these Terms of Sale 
and Stack’s Bowers reserves the right to require a written 
guarantee of such payments and obligations. Bidders who 
have not established credit with Stack’s Bowers must fur-
nish satisfactory information and credit references and/
or deposit at least twenty-five percent (25%) of their total 
bids for that Auction Sale session(s) or such other amount 
as Stack’s Bowers may, in its sole and absolute discretion 
require before any bids from such Bidder will be accepted. 
Deposits submitted will be applied to purchases. Any re -
maining deposits will be promptly refunded, upon clear -
ance of funds.
 Bid Online at www.StacksBowers.com Pa ge 261

========== PAGE 280 ==========

TERMS AND CONDITIONS OF SALE
7.  Sales Tax
United States Buyers will be charged all applicable sales 
tax unless a valid Resale Certificate has been provided to 
the Auctioneer prior to the auction. Should state sales tax 
become applicable in the delivery state prior to delivery 
of the property on the invoice, the United States Buyers 
agree to pay all applicable state sales tax as required by 
the delivery state as of the shipping date. In the event any 
applicable sales tax is not paid by a United States Buyer 
that should have been paid, even if not such tax was not 
charged or collected by Stack’s Bowers by mistake, error, 
negligence or gross negligence, a United States Buyer 
nonetheless acknowledges responsibility to pay such sales 
tax and remains fully liable for and agrees to promptly 
pay such taxes on demand, together with any interest or 
penalty that may be assessed by the taxing authority and 
agrees to indemnify and hold Auctioneer harmless from 
any applicable sales tax, interest or penalties due. Lots 
from different Auctions may not be aggregated for sales 
tax purposes.
8.  Financial Responsibility
In the event any applicable conditions of these Terms of 
Sale herein are not complied with by a Buyer or if the 
Buyer fails to make payment in full by the Default Date, 
Stack’s Bowers reserves the right, in its sole discretion, in 
addition to all other remedies which it may have at law or 
in equity to rescind the sale of that lot or any other lot or 
lots sold to the defaulting Buyer, retaining all payments 
made by Buyer as liquidated damages, it being recognized 
that actual damages may be speculative or difficult to 
compute, and upon prior notice to Buyer resell a portion 
or all of the lots held by Stack’s Bowers, in a commercially 
reasonable manner, which may include a public or private 
sale, in a quantity sufficient in the sole opinion of Stack’s 
Bowers to satisfy the indebtedness, plus all accrued charg-
es, and Stack’s Bowers may charge a seller’s commission 
that is commercially reasonable. More than one such sale 
may take place at the option of Stack’s Bowers. If Stack’s 
Bowers resells the lots, Buyer agrees to pay for the reason-
able cost of such sale,. Notice of the sale shall be by inter-
nationally recognized overnight courier service to the ad-
dress utilized on the Bid Sheet, Auction Consignment and 
Security Agreement or other last known address by Stack’s 
Bowers. The proceeds shall be applied first to the satisfac-
tion of any damages occasioned by Buyer’s breach, then 
to any other indebtedness owed to Stack’s Bowers, includ-
ing without limitation, commissions, handling charges, 
carrying charges, the expenses of both sales, seller’s fees, 
reasonable attorneys’ fees, costs, collection agency fees 
and costs and any other costs or expenses incurred. Buyer 
may also be liable to Stack’s Bowers for any deficiency if 
the proceeds of such sale or sales are insufficient to cover 
such amounts.
Buyer grants to Stack’s Bowers, its affiliates and assign -
ees, the right to offset any sums due, or found to be due 
to Stack’s Bowers, and to make such offset from any any 
sums due to Buyer by Stack’s Bowers, its affiliates and as-
signees. In addition, defaulting Buyers will be deemed to 
have granted to Stack’s Bowers, its affiliates and assignees, 
a security interest in: (x) the purchased lots and their pro-
ceeds, and (y) such sums or other items and their pro -
ceeds, in the possession of Stack’s Bowers, its affiliates or 
assignees, to secure all indebtedness due to Stack’s Bow -
ers and its affiliated companies, plus all accrued expenses, 
carrying charges, seller’s fees, attorney fees, and costs, un-
til the indebtedness is paid in full. A United States Buyer 
grants Stack’s Bowers the right to file a UCC-1 financing 
statement for such items. Non-United States Buyers grant 
to Stack’s Bowers such other form of as may be required 
to perfect or otherwise confirm a security interest therein. 
Stack’s Bowers may assign any such interest to any affili-
ated or related company or any third party deemed appro-
priate by Stack’s Bowers without notice or consent of the 
Buyer. If the auction invoice is not paid for in full by the 
Default Date, a carrying charge of one-and-one-half per-
cent (1-1/2%) per month may be imposed on the unpaid 
amount until it is paid in full. In the event this interest 
rate exceeds the interest permitted by law, the same shall 
be adjusted to the maximum rate permitted by law, and 
any amount paid in excess thereof shall be allocated to 
principal. Where permitted under applicable law, Buyer 
agrees to pay all reasonable attorney’s fees, court costs and 
other collection costs incurred by Stack’s Bowers or any 
affiliated or related company to collect past due invoices 
or to interpret or enforce the terms hereof or in any ac -
tion or proceeding arising out of or related to the Auction 
Sale. Stack’s Bowers reserves the right to assign its interest 
to any third party. To the extent that the Buyer for any 
lot consists of more than one person or entity, each such 
person or entity is jointly and severally liable for all obli -
gations of the Buyer, regardless of the title or capacity of 
such person or entity. With respect to United States Buy-
ers, Stack’s Bowers shall have all the rights of a secured 
creditor under Article 9 of the California Commercial 
Code and with respect to non-United States Buyers such 
other applicable law, and all rights of the consignor to col-
lect amounts due from the Buyer, whether at law or equity.
9.  Shipping
It is the Buyer’s responsibility to contact Stack’s Bowers 
after the sale to make shipping and packaging arrange -
ments. Due to the fragile nature of some lots, Stack’s Bow-
ers may elect not to assume responsibility for shipping or 
packing, or may charge additional shipping and handling. 
Stack’s Bowers, in its sole discretion, may not ship to se -
lect countries. Lots indicated as being “framed” or that are 
specifically identified in the catalog are shipped at Buyer’s 
risk. All taxes, postage, shipping, if applicable, handling, 
insurance costs, the Buyer’s Premium, and any other fees 
required by law to be charged or collected will be added to 
the invoice for any lots invoiced to Buyer. All lots shipped 
to countries outside of the country where the Auction 
is hosted will be billed an additional one-half percent 
(1/2%) for insurance (minimum of €10). For any lots de-
livered outside the country where the auction is hosted, 
the declaration value shall be the item’(s) hammer price 
plus its buyer’s premium. Auctioneer shall not be liable 
for any loss caused or resulting from seizure or destruc -
tion under quarantine or customs regulation or confis -
cation by order of any government or public authority. 
Buyer shall be responsible for paying all applicable taxes, 
Page 262 Stack’s Bowers Galleries

========== PAGE 281 ==========

TERMS AND CONDITIONS OF SALE
duties and customs charges for all lots delivered outside 
the country where the auction is hosted. All lots will be 
shipped FOB Destination, freight prepaid and charged 
back. Title and risk of loss pass to the Buyer at the destina-
tion upon tender of delivery. Acceptance of delivery con-
stitutes acceptance of the purchased lots. Inspection of the 
purchased lots is not required for acceptance. Any and all 
claims based upon Buyer’s failure to receive a purchased 
lot, Buyer’s receipt of a lot in damaged condition, or oth-
erwise related to delivery, must be received in writing by 
Stack’s Bowers no later than the earlier of thirty (30) days 
after payment, or the date of the Auction Sale (the “Out -
side Claim Date”). As Buyers may not receive notification 
of shipment, it is Buyer’s responsibility to keep track of 
the Outside Claim Date and make timely notification of 
any such claim. The failure to make a timely claim, time 
being of the essence, shall constitute a waiver of any such 
claim. Orders paid by credit card will only be shipped to 
the verified address on file with the credit card merchant.
It shall be the responsibility for Buyer to arrange pick-up 
or shipping in a timely manner (within 10 days). All Lots 
left at our facility or under our control after an Auction 
Sale for more than 60 days will be sent to secure storage 
and incur a storage fee in the amount of €10.00 per item 
per day starting upon our written notice to you and con -
tinuing until the Lots are retrieved. Ninety (90) days af -
ter the date of such notice, if the Lots have not yet been 
retrieved, we may sell the Lots in a commercially rea -
sonable manner, which may include a public or private 
sale, in a quantity sufficient in the opinion of Auctioneer 
to satisfy any unpaid amounts, plus all accrued charges, 
and Auctioneer may charge a seller’s commission that is 
commercially reasonable. More than one such sale may 
take place at the option of Auctioneer. The net proceeds 
of such sale shall then be applied to the satisfaction of any 
unpaid amounts, including the costs of storage and sale 
(including reasonable expenses and attorney’s fee) and 
the excess, if any, will be remitted to you, held for your 
account or disposed of as required by law.
10. DISCLAIMER AND W ARRANTIES
NO W ARRANTY OF MERCHANTABILITY OR FIT -
NESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE IS MADE OR 
IMPLIED ON ANY LOT. NO W ARRANTY , WHETHER 
EXPRESSED OR IMPLIED, IS MADE WITH RESPECT 
TO ANY LOT EXCEPT FOR W ARRANTY OF TITLE 
PROVIDED BY THE CONSIGNOR. AUCTIONEER 
IS SELLING ONLY THAT RIGHT OR TITLE TO THE 
LOT THAT THE CONSIGNOR MAY HAVE AS OF THE 
AUCTION SALE DATE. ALL LOTS ARE SOLD “ AS IS” 
AND WITH ALL FAULTS. PURCHASER HEREBY AS-
SUMES ALL RISKS CONCERNING AND RELATED 
TO THE GRADING, QUALITY , DESCRIPTION, CON-
DITION, AND PROVENANCE OF A LOT.
a. COINS, CURRENCY AND CRYPTOCURRENCY 
TOKENS LISTED IN THIS CATALOG AS GRADED 
AND ENCAPSULATED BY PCGS, NGC, CAC GRAD -
ING, ANACS, ICG, PCGS CURRENCY , PMG, PCGS 
BANKNOTE GRADING, CMC OR ANY OTHER 
THIRD PARTY GRADING SERVICE ARE SOLD “ AS-
IS” EXCEPT AS EXPRESSLY SET FORTH HEREIN 
AND MAY NOT BE RETURNED FOR ANY REASON 
WHATSOEVER BY ANY BUYER. ALL THIRD PARTY 
GRADING SERVICE GUARANTEES, INCLUDING 
AUTHENTICITY , ARE THE SOLE RESPONSIBILITY 
OF THE THIRD PARTY GRADING SERVICE AND 
NOT W ARRANTIES OR GUARANTEES OF THE 
AUCTIONEER. BUYERS SHOULD CONTACT THESE 
THIRD PARTY GRADING SERVICES DIRECTLY 
WITH RESPECT TO ANY CLAIMS OR QUESTIONS 
THEY MAY HAVE CONCERNING THEIR GUARAN -
TEES AND W ARRANTIES. BUYERS ACKNOWLEDGE 
AND AGREE THAT AUCTIONEER IS NOT BOUND 
BY OR LIABLE FOR ANY OPINION OR CERTIFCA -
TION BY ANY THIRD PARTY GRADING SERVICE.
b. In the case of non-certified coins, currency and cryp -
tocurrency tokens that have neither been examined by 
the Buyer prior to the Auction Sale, nor purchased by the 
Buyer or Buyer’s agent at the Auction Sale, if it is deter -
mined in a review by Stack’s Bowers that there is a ma -
terial error in the catalog description of a non-certified 
coin, currency, or cryptocurrency token such lot may be 
returned, provided written notice is received by Stack’s 
Bowers no later than seventy-two (72) hours of delivery 
of the lots in question, and such lots are returned and 
received by Stack’s Bowers, in their original, sealed con -
tainers, no later than fourteen (14) calendar days after de-
livery, in the same condition the lot(s) were delivered to 
the Buyer, time being of the essence. Non-certified coins, 
currency and cryptocurrency tokens that have been either 
examined by the Buyer prior to the Auction Sale or pur -
chased by the Buyer or Buyer’s agent at the Auction Sale, 
are not granted return privileges, except for authenticity 
(unless described and sold as not authentic).
c. All non-certified coins and currency are guaranteed to 
be authentic.
d. All certified and non-certified cryptocurrency tokens 
are guaranteed to be authentic only. Auctioneer disclaims 
any guaranty of any kind with respect to cryptocurrency 
tokens, including, but not limited to: face value, the con -
tents or existence of any accounts, wallets, or other physi-
cal, digital or other receptacles of value, the existence of a 
cryptographic private key, or the ability to fund any cryp-
tocurrency. Actual cryptocurrency value is neither con-
firmed nor guaranteed by Auctioneer.
If an item or items are returned pursuant to the terms 
herein, they must be housed in their original, sealed and 
unopened container.
e. Late remittance or removal of any item from its original 
container or third party graded holder, or altering a coin 
constitutes just cause for revocation of all return privi
-
leges.
f. Grading or condition of rare coins, currency and cryp-
tocurrency tokens may have a material effect on the value 
of the item(s) purchased, and the opinion of others (in -
cluding independent grading services) may differ with the 
independent grading services opinion or interpretation of 
Stack’s Bowers. Stack’s Bowers shall not be bound by any 
prior, or subsequent opinion, determination or certifica -
tion by any independent grading service.
